data_quality_notes: SUBSTITUTION: True habitat fragmentation metrics (patch size, edge density, core area index) are not available as country-level statistics in any accessible API — they require raster processing of MODIS/ESA land cover data. Used 'Agricultural land (% of land area)' (WB: AG.LND.AGRI.ZS) as an approximate proxy: higher agricultural land share implies greater habitat conversion and fragmentation. This is an indirect proxy that captures land-use pressure rather than landscape configuration directly. Already-used proxies excluded: LBII, SHI, IUCN Red List, protected areas, FAO pesticides, PARC indices. n=5892 observations, 177 countries after merge.
summary: Using agricultural land area (% of total land) as an indirect proxy for habitat fragmentation (higher agricultural expansion → more habitat conversion and fragmentation → lower SPI). Bivariate Pearson r=-0.039 (p=2.480e-03), Spearman rho=-0.021 (p=1.113e-01). Partial r=-0.031 (p=2.158e-02) controlling for log(GDP/capita). Best functional form: quadratic. Verdict: partially_confirmed.
